original: Lásló Jósef Bíró, Hungarian inventor (ballpoint pen), born in Budapest, Hungary (d. 1985)
Telephone Answering Machine created by Bell Laboratories
NY Giants'Willie Maysmakes a famous over-the-shoulder catch of Cleveland Indians' Vic Wertz's 460' drive during Game One of the World Series at the Polo Grounds in NYC
Stan Berenstain, American children's author (The Berenstain Bears), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 2005)
American astronomer Seth Barnes Nicholson discovers Ananke, 12th moon of Jupiter from Mount Wilson Observatory, Los Angeles County, California

Gene Autry, American cowboy singer, songwriter, actor, musician, ("Back In The Saddle Again"; "Rudolph The Red-Nosed Reindeer") and business tycoon (owner MLB Angels team, 1961-97), born near Tioga, Texas (d. 1998)
Great auroral display in the US
Battle of Salamis: The Greek fleet under Themistocles defeats the Persian fleet under Xerxes I
Caroline Yale, American educator who revolutionized the teaching of hearing-impaired students, born in Charlotte, Vermont (d. 1943)
Chuck Cooper, American shooting guard and 1st African American to be drafted into the NBA (Boston Celtics), born in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania (d. 1984)
Nancy Leftenant-Colon, American Army nurse and major, first Black woman to join US Army Corps in 1946, born in Goose Creek, South Carolina (d. 2025)[1]
Madeline Kahn, American stage and screen actress (What's Up, Doc?; Young Frankenstein; Paper Moon; Blazing Saddles), and singer, born in Boston, Massachusetts (d. 1999)
Jon Brower Minnoch, American, world's heaviest recorded man at 1,400lb (635 kilograms; 100 stone), born in Bainbridge Island, Washington recorded man (d. 1983)

Mike Post [Leland Michael Postil], American Grammy and Emmy award-winning television composer (Rockford Files; Hill Street Blues; The Greatest American Hero, Magnum, P.I.), and producer (Kenny Rogers; Van Halen III), born in Berkley, California
Williamsburg Bray School, now the oldest extant building dedicated to the education of Black children in the United States, opens in Williamsburg, Virginia
1st US merchant ship "Booker T. Washington" commanded by a black captain (Hugh Mulzac), launched at Wilmington Delaware
Rainer Weiss, German-American physicist known for his work on the LIGO detector (Nobel Prize, 2017), born in Berlin, Germany (d. 2025)[1]
Launch of Alouette 1, 1st Canadian satellite (on US Delta rocket)
First practical public electric tramway in the world is opened in Blackpool, England
Steinhart Aquarium opens to public in Golden Gate Park, San Francisco, California
Maureen Caird, Australian hurdler (youngest to win individual Olympic Gold at 17 in 1968), born in Cumberland, Australia
Emile Zatopek runs world record 20,000 m & 10 mile
Yankees sluggerBabe Ruthsets then-MLB home run season record at 54 with a 9th inning shot off Dave Keefe in New York's 7-3 win at the Philadelphia A's
YanksMickey Mantlehits his 52nd HR of season
Yankees sluggerBabe Ruthties MLB record by hitting grand slams in consecutive games; New York's 7-4 win v Philadelphia A's and 15-4 rout of Washington Senators
Eddie Tolan, American sprinter (2 Golds 1932 Olympics), born in Denver, Colorado (d. 1967)
WWI: Allied forces score a decisive breakthrough of Hindenburg Line
Joe LouisTKOs Lou Nova in 6 for heavyweight boxing title
Rolling Stones 1st tour, as opening act forBo Diddley& the Everly Brothers
Gladys Knight& the Pips are the first to release Norman Whitfield and Barrett Strong's "I Heard It Through The Grapevine"
Dizzy Gillespie presents his 1st Carnegie Hall concert, NYC
Musical film "Strike Up the Band" starringMickey RooneyandJudy Garlandopens
First units of the London Metropolitan Police appear on the streets of the British capital, the city's first modern police force
Hurricane claims 275 in Mississippi Delta
Orbiting Solar Observatory VII launched
Chuck Latourette, sets NFL record 47.7 yd punt return avg (3 punts)
1st time NL pennant ends in a tie (Cards & Dodgers)
Little Anthony & the Imperials record "Shimmy Shimmy Ko Ko Bop"
Mickey Hart joins the Grateful Dead for the 1st time, playing at the Straight Theater in San Francisco, California
Los Angeles (previously Cleveland) Rams play 1st NFL game in LA
1st Canadian football game played under lights, Hamilton-UBC
Fidel LaBarba, American boxer (Olympic gold flyweight 1924; world flyweight title 1927), born in New York City (d. 1981)
James Cronin, American nuclear physicist (Physics Nobel Prize 1980), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 2016)
Bill Nelson, American astronaut (STS 61C), attorney,and politician (U.S. Representative (D) Florida, 1979-1999; U.S. Senate (D) Florida, 2001-19; NASA Administrator, 2021- ), born in Miami, Florida

Tommy Boyce, American songwriter (with Bobby Hart: The Monkees - "Last Train to Clarksville"; (I'm Not Your) Steppin' Stone"), and singer (Boyce & Hart - "I Wonder What She's Doing Tonight"), born in Charlottesville, Virginia (d. 1994)
Jean-Luc Ponty, French jazz-fusion electric violinist (Frank Zappa; Mahavishnu Orchestra; Cosmic Messenger; Tchokola), born in Avranches, France
Felice Gimondi, Italian road cyclist (Tour de France 1965; Giro d'Italia 1967, 69, 76; Vuelta a España 1968), born in Sedrina, Italy (d. 2019)
Rajinder Goel, Indian cricket spin bowler (record for most wickets in Ranji Trophy 637), born in Narwana, British India (d. 2020)
Peter D. Mitchell, English chemist, (1978 Nobel Prize for Chemistry), born in Mitcham, England (d. 1992)
Mehmood [Ali], Indian actor, singer, director and producer (Do Phoo!, Vardaan), born in Bombay, British India (d. 2004)
Mihály Csíkszentmihályi, Hungarian- American psychologist (concept of "flow"), born in Rijeka, Kingdom of Italy (d. 2021)
Mark Farner, American musician, songwriter and vocalist (Grand Funk Railroad), born in Flint, Michigan
Mercator Cooper, American sea captain (first to visit Japan and Antarctica), born in Southampton, New York (d. 1872)
Caravaggio [Michelangelo Merisi da Caravaggio], Italian painter (Saint Francis of Assisi in Ecstasy) [birthdate disputed], born in Milan (d. 1610)
Tintoretto, Italian Mannerist painter and notable exponent of the Renaissance school, born in Venice, Republic of Venice (d. 1594)
Joan of Kent [the Fair Maid of Kent], famous English beauty, wife of Edward the Black Prince, mother of Richard II, born at Palace of Woodstock, England (d. 1385)
English expedition to Hudson Bay led by Frenchman Médard Chouart Des Groseilliers arrives at Nemiscau (Rupert) River aboard the Nonsuch (beginning of Hudson Bay Company)
Battle of Pákozd: Hungarian forces defeat Croats at Pákozd; the first battle of the War of Independence
French Government in exile of De Gaulle cancels agreement of Munich
Construction begins on Washington National Cathedral
